_meta.keyKnowledge: We explore the diversity of music by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander musicians and consider culturally appropriate approaches.

_source.w: We explore the diversity of music by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander musicians and consider culturally appropriate approaches.
y: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander music encompasses extraordinary diversity across hundreds of distinct nations and traditions.
Culturally appropriate engagement ensures we approach First Nations music with the respect it deserves.
Understanding these musical traditions challenges stereotypes and reveals the depth of the world's oldest continuous cultures.
eg: We listen to and explore music by a diverse range of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander musicians.
We learn about cultural protocols for engaging with First Nations music and performance.
We investigate how contemporary First Nations musicians blend traditional and modern elements.
We discuss the importance of cultural ownership and appropriate use of First Nations music.
We explore how music connects to Country, language, ceremony and community.
We engage with resources created by Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander musicians about their practice.
